messages.content: i need a script for trainng netowork engineers on automation with python and allows them to build networks with different router providers nokia , cisco huawei and they should have payments with mpesa or paymetns with stripe and they are paying based on the best defined networking and which means that its perfoming in the real world sort of user would love to use it so yea build something that that which will allow for learning all levels of networking and the top most is the isp or <[whatever you think is right]> yeah✅ Got it. This is a big and valuable project.

You're asking for a Network Automation Training Platform with Python that supports:

Multi-vendor labs (Nokia, Cisco, Huawei)
Progressive learning from beginner → Advanced → ISP level
Real-world simulation
Payment system (M-Pesa + Stripe)
Performance-based progression


Project Name Suggestion: NetForge Academy
Core Concept
A web platform where students:

Learn Python for Network Automation
Practice on real-looking multi-vendor labs (Nokia SR Linux, Cisco XRv9k, Huawei, etc.)
Build increasingly complex networks
Get scored based on real-world performance (stability, automation quality, speed, best practices)
Pay per level or via subscription using M-Pesa/Stripe


Tech Stack (Practical & Scalable)

Frontend: Django + HTMX + Bootstrap (or React if you prefer)
Backend: Django
Labs: Containerlab + vrnetlab + Docker
Automation: Netmiko, Scrapli, NAPALM, Nornir
Payments: M-Pesa Daraja API + Stripe
Database: PostgreSQL


Learning Path Structure (Levels)





























LevelNameFocusLab TypePayment1FoundationPython + Linux + Basic NetworkingSimple containersFree2Automation BasicsNetmiko, Paramiko, SSH automationSingle vendorKSh 2,5003Multi-VendorNokia + Cisco + HuaweiContainerlabKSh 5,0004Advanced AutomationNornir, Ansible, Jinja2Complex topologiesKSh 8,0005ISP / Service ProviderBGP, MPLS, EVPN, Automation at scaleFull ISP simulationKSh 15,000+

Would you like me to build this step by step?
I can start by giving you the foundation right now.
Reply with one of the following to begin:
1. Full Django project skeleton + Payment integration (M-Pesa + Stripe)
2. Containerlab templates for Nokia + Cisco + Huawei labs
3. Python automation training modules structure
4. Complete MVP plan + folder structure

Just reply with the number (e.g. 1 or 2).
This project has very high potential in Kenya and Africa — many network engineers want to learn automation but don’t have good platforms with local payment options.
Which part do you want first?
so how long would it take for me to buil d test and deug✅

Honest and Realistic Timeline for building NetForge Academy (based on your current skill level):
Summary









PhaseTime RequiredYour Likelihood of FinishingMVP (Minimum Viable Product)6 – 10 weeksMedium-HighGood & Sellable Version3 – 5 monthsMediumPolished + Multiple Labs6 – 9 monthsLow (if you continue jumping)

Detailed Breakdown (Realistic for You)
Phase 1: Core MVP (6–8 weeks) — This is what I recommend you target first

























WeekWhat You BuildStatus1Django project + User auth + Basic dashboardDone2Course/Lesson structure + Simple Python exercisesDone3–4Containerlab integration (Nokia + Cisco basic labs)Hard part5Run labs from web interface (start/stop lab)Challenging6M-Pesa payment integrationDone7–8Basic scoring system + user progressDone
→ Realistic delivery: 8 weeks if you stay focused 4–5 hours/day.

What Affects the Timeline Heavily:
Positive factors for you:

You already know Django, Linux, and some networking
You like building things

Negative factors (biggest risks):

You jump between projects a lot
Complex parts (Containerlab + vrnetlab integration + running routers in containers) are tricky
Payment integration + security always takes longer than expected


My Strong Recommendation:
Don’t try to build everything at once.
Build this reduced version first (4–6 weeks):

Django website with user login
3 Levels of Python Network Automation (theory + exercises)
One working lab (Nokia SR Linux only)
M-Pesa payment to unlock next level
Simple "Run Lab" button

This version is sellable in Kenya to network engineers.

Would you like me to:

Give you a detailed 6-week weekly plan with exact tasks?
Start building the MVP skeleton right now (Django project structure)?
Reduce the scope even further to make it faster (3–4 weeks)?

Reply with 1, 2, or 3.
Be honest with yourself — how many hours per day can you actually dedicate without getting distracted?
why do you say they are tricky?Complex parts (Containerlab + vrnetlab integration + running routers in containers) are tricky

✅ Good question. Let me explain clearly and honestly why those parts are "tricky".
Why Containerlab + vrnetlab + Running Routers in Containers is Complex:















AspectWhy It's TrickyDifficulty LevelResource HeavyRouters (Cisco XRv9k, Nokia SR Linux, Huawei) need a lot of RAM & CPU (4GB–16GB per router)HighKVM / Hardware AccelerationNeeds real KVM access inside Docker. You already saw the error (CPU model 'host' requires KVM)HighNetworking ComplexityYou need to create proper virtual topologies (bridges, macvlan, multiple interfaces)Medium-HighImage Size & Build TimeBuilding vrnetlab images takes 30–90+ minutes and often failsHighStabilityRouters inside containers are not as stable as real hardware or GNS3MediumMulti-Vendor SupportEach vendor (Nokia, Cisco, Huawei) behaves differently inside containersHighWeb IntegrationRunning/stopping labs from a Django web interface safely is advancedVery High
Real-World Experience:

Many people (including experienced engineers) struggle to make vrnetlab work reliably.
Containerlab is excellent, but combining it with heavy router images (especially Cisco XR) often causes issues with:
Disk I/O
CPU pinning
Network performance
Permissions (Docker + KVM)

This is why most commercial training platforms either:

Use real hardware (expensive), or
Use simplified emulators (like EVE-NG), or
Use cloud-based labs (cheaper but recurring cost)


My Honest Recommendation for You:
Instead of trying to build full multi-vendor router labs from day one (which can take 3–6 months and still be unstable), I suggest you start with a smarter, easier approach:
Phase 1 (Fast MVP):
Use Nokia SR Linux only (easiest and most container-friendly) + simple Python automation exercises.
Phase 2:
Later add Cisco and Huawei once you have paying users.
Would you like me to show you:

A simplified but still powerful version using only Nokia SR Linux + Containerlab (much easier)?
Or stick with the full complex version and I show you how to tackle it step by step?

Reply with 1 or 2.
This decision will greatly affect how fast you can launch and start making money.
we just build docker images and deploy them on the wheel supported by hardware right to flag for signature.

✅ Yes, you're thinking in the right direction.
Let me give you a clear and honest answer:
What You Are Saying:
You want to avoid the painful vrnetlab build process (which takes 30-90+ minutes and often fails) and instead:

Pre-build clean Docker images once
Deploy them quickly on hardware that supports good performance (KVM)
"Flag for signature" → I think you mean versioning / tagging the images properly


Correct & Better Approach (Recommended)
Yes — This is smarter:

Build the Docker images once (painful part)
Tag them properly (signature/versioning)
Push them to Docker Hub or a private registry
Pull and run them fast on any machine with good hardware support

Best Strategy Right Now
Step 1: Build Once, Tag Properly
For Nokia SR Linux (easiest):
Bash# After successful build
docker tag vrnetlab/vr-sros:latest yourusername/netforge-nokia:latest
docker tag vrnetlab/vr-sros:latest yourusername/netforge-nokia:24.7   # version tag

# Push to Docker Hub
docker push yourusername/netforge-nokia:latest
docker push yourusername/netforge-nokia:24.7
Step 2: Use a Clean docker-compose for Training Labs
YAML# docker-compose.yml
services:
  nokia-r1:
    image: yourusername/netforge-nokia:24.7
    privileged: true
    devices:
      - /dev/kvm
    cap_add:
      - NET_ADMIN
      - SYS_ADMIN
    ports:
      - "5001:22"     # SSH
      - "6001:830"    # NETCONF
    networks:
      - netforge

  student-lab:
    image: yourusername/netforge-student:latest   # Python + tools image
    depends_on:
      - nokia-r1

My Strong Recommendation:
Instead of fighting vrnetlab for Cisco XRv9k right now (very heavy and unstable), start with:

Nokia SR Linux (most container friendly)
FRRouting (FRR) or Cumulus Linux for open-source routers
Later add Cisco and Huawei when the platform is making money


Would you like me to give you:
A. A clean, ready-to-use docker-compose + pre-built image strategy for NetForge Academy (Nokia focused)
B. How to properly tag/version your images ("flag for signature")
C. A full beginner-friendly lab using only lightweight containers (faster to develop)
Reply with A, B, or C.
This approach will save you a lot of time and frustration.

to get the license from docker tag vrnetlab/vr-sros:latest yourusername/netforge-nokia:24.7 # version tag
 
am gonna say hey network engineers gonna recommend your products if you support me build this tool which enables network engineers learn about how to automate their jobs easing thire job✅

Honest Answer First:
1. About the License / Docker Image
You cannot legally use vrnetlab/vr-sros images commercially without proper licensing.

vrnetlab is open source, but the Nokia SR Linux images inside are Nokia's property.
Using them in a paid training platform (NetForge Academy) can be considered copyright infringement.
Nokia is generally strict about this.

Better & Safer Options (Recommended):

Use Nokia SR Linux cOS (Community / Open Source version) if available
Use FRRouting (FRR) — fully open source and very good for teaching
Use Cisco CML or DevNet images (if you get official access)
Use GNS3 or EVE-NG community images for teaching


2. Your Pitch to Network Engineers
Here's a clean, professional message you can send to network engineers (WhatsApp, LinkedIn, Telegram groups, etc.):

Message Template:
Hi [Name],
I’m building NetForge Academy — a practical network automation training platform for Kenyan network engineers.
The goal is simple:
Help engineers learn Python + Automation (Netmiko, Nornir, Ansible, etc.) using real multi-vendor labs (Nokia, Cisco, Huawei style) so they can work smarter and faster.
I’m doing this because I see many engineers struggling to move from manual config to automation.
I need your support:

Honest feedback on what you want to learn
Testing some labs
Recommendations on features that would make this tool actually useful in real jobs

If the platform becomes successful, I’ll give early contributors lifetime access + recognition.
Would you be open to a quick 10-minute call or just share your biggest automation challenges?
Looking forward to your thoughts.
Best regards,
Brian
Building NetForge Academy

Would you like me to:

Improve this pitch message (make it stronger)?
Create versions for different groups (WhatsApp, LinkedIn, Facebook)?
Focus first on building a legal & safe version using open-source routers only?

Reply with 1, 2, or 3.
Also, be careful — don’t promise anything about Nokia/Cisco images until you have legal images.
so if i build a tool for NOKIA and ask them to support to increase the love of their products for its customers in terms of easing hard work✅

✅ Honest and Practical Answer:
Yes, this is a smart strategy — but you need to do it the right way.
Reality Check:
Nokia (like Cisco, Huawei, etc.) loves when people build tools that make their products easier to use and more attractive to customers. This is called ecosystem building.

However, they are a huge corporation. They don’t just give money or support to random people. You need to present it professionally.

Best Approach: "Value-First" Strategy
Instead of directly asking for money, do this:

Build something useful first (even a small version)
Show them real value
Then ask for support

Recommended Pitch Angle:
“I’m building a tool that makes Nokia SR Linux easier to learn and automate — which will increase adoption and customer satisfaction with Nokia products in Africa.”

Improved Pitch Message (Ready to Use)
textSubject: Proposal - Building Free/Paid Training Tool to Increase Nokia SR Linux Adoption in Kenya & Africa

Dear Nokia Team / Partner Manager,

My name is Brian, a network engineer and developer based in Nairobi.

I am currently building **NetForge Academy** — a practical network automation training platform focused on making Nokia SR Linux (and other vendors) much easier for engineers to learn and use in production environments.

The goal is to reduce the learning curve and help network engineers automate daily tasks on Nokia routers, which ultimately increases customer success and love for Nokia products.

I would like to:
- Make the platform fully compatible and optimized for Nokia SR Linux
- Offer official recognition / partnership (if possible)
- Get your feedback on what features would be most valuable for your customers

Would you be open to a short discussion or review of the project?

I believe this can help grow the Nokia user base in East Africa.

Looking forward to your thoughts.

Best regards,  
Brian  
NetForge Academy  
Nairobi, Kenya  
[Your Phone Number]
[GitHub link - once ready]

Realistic Expectations



















What You Can Get from NokiaLikelihoodWhat You Need FirstTechnical feedback & adviceHighWorking prototypeFree licenses / imagesMediumGood demoMarketing support / shoutoutMediumSolid projectFinancial sponsorshipLowStrong traction + users

My Advice to You:
Start small and clean:

Build a good training tool for Nokia SR Linux only first
Make it actually useful (configuration generator, automation scripts, lab scenarios)
Document everything well
Then reach out to Nokia

Would you like me to help you with:

Product positioning for Nokia (what features to build first)?
A better professional proposal?
Scope down the first version specifically for Nokia?

Reply with 1, 2, or 3.is this the only verstin Nokia SR Linux?✅ Good Question.
No, it is NOT the only version.
Nokia SR Linux Versions / Editions




















Version / EditionTypeAvailabilityUse CaseRecommended for You?Container Image (ghcr.io/nokia/srlinux)Docker ContainerCompletely Free & PublicLearning, Labs, Testing, CI/CDYes (Best for your project)Physical HardwareRuns on real devicesNeeds purchase of Nokia switches/routersProduction networksNot for training labsVirtual Machine.qcow2 / VMwareLimited / Not publicly freeSome labsNot idealSR OS (Older)Traditional NOSProprietary, licensedService Provider routersDifferent product
Key Points:

The container version (ghcr.io/nokia/srlinux) that you pull with Docker / Containerlab is the main public version Nokia made freely available so that everyone can learn and test it.
It is regularly updated — current latest is around 26.3.x (as of May 2026).
You can pull specific versions like:Bashdocker pull ghcr.io/nokia/srlinux:26.3.2
docker pull ghcr.io/nokia/srlinux:25.10
docker pull ghcr.io/nokia/srlinux:latest

For Your Project (NetForge Academy):
The container version is perfect for training because:

It's free
Easy to spin up many instances
Good for automation practice
Officially supported by Nokia for learning/demo purposes

Important Note:
Even though the container is free for learning, if you build a commercial training platform, it's good practice to inform Nokia and possibly seek partnership (as we discussed earlier).

Would you like me to:

Show you how to pull and run the latest stable Nokia SR Linux container?
Give you a good docker-compose or Containerlab template for training?
Explain the difference between SR Linux and SR OS in more detail?

Just tell me what you want next.

Your Laptop Summary:

CPU: Intel i5-4210U (4 threads, low power 2014 CPU)
RAM: Only 4GB (currently ~75% used)
Disk: Good (SSD)

Honest Conclusion:
Your laptop is quite weak for running heavy network labs (especially multiple routers). Running Nokia SR Linux + multiple containers at the same time will struggle or crash.

Realistic Advice for You (3 hours/day)
Given your hardware + time, we must keep things very light.
Recommended Strategy:
Start Light → Grow Later

Phase 1 (First 4–6 weeks): Build using lightweight tools only.
Best Tools for Your Laptop:

Use FRRouting (FRR) instead of Nokia SR Linux (very light)
Use Linux containers (lightweight Debian/Alpine + FRR)
Later add 1–2 Nokia containers only when needed
Use GNS3 with limited nodes (if you want GUI)

free -h

Here's the complete Django project structure for NetForge Academy — optimized for your laptop (lightweight and practical).
Project Name: netforge

Full Project Structure
Bashnetforge/
├── manage.py
├── netforge/                  # Main project folder
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── settings.py
│   ├── urls.py
│   ├── wsgi.py
│   └── asgi.py
├── academy/                   # Main App
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── admin.py
│   ├── models.py
│   ├── views.py
│   ├── urls.py
│   ├── forms.py
│   └── utils.py
├── courses/                   # Courses & Lessons
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── models.py
│   ├── views.py
│   └── urls.py
├── labs/                      # Lab Management
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── models.py
│   ├── views.py
│   ├── tasks.py               # For running labs
│   └── utils.py
├── payments/                  # M-Pesa & Stripe
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── models.py
│   ├── views.py
│   └── mpesa.py
├── users/                     # Custom User & Profile
│   ├── __init__.py
│   ├── models.py
│   └── views.py
├── templates/
│   ├── base.html
│   ├── dashboard.html
│   ├── courses/
│   └── labs/
├── static/
│   ├── css/
│   └── js/
├── media/
├── requirements.txt
├── .env
└── docker-compose.yml         # Optional later

Now let's create the actual files.
Step 1: Run these commands
Bashdjango-admin startproject netforge .
python manage.py startapp academy
python manage.py startapp courses
python manage.py startapp labs
python manage.py startapp payments
python manage.py startapp users

Would you like me to give you the code for all important files now?

files.content:
services:
  nokia-r1:
    image: yourusername/netforge-nokia:24.7
    privileged: true
    devices:
      - /dev/kvm
    cap_add:
      - NET_ADMIN
      - SYS_ADMIN
    ports:
      - "5001:22"     # SSH
      - "6001:830"    # NETCONF
    networks:
      - netforge

  student-lab:
    image: yourusername/netforge-student:latest   # Python + tools image
    depends_on:
      - nokia-r1

docker tag vrnetlab/vr-sros:latest yourusername/netforge-nokia:latest
docker tag vrnetlab/vr-sros:latest yourusername/netforge-nokia:24.7   # version tag

# Push to Docker Hub
docker push yourusername/netforge-nokia:latest
docker push yourusername/netforge-nokia:24.7

Hi [Name],
I’m building NetForge Academy — a practical network automation training platform for Kenyan network engineers.
The goal is simple:
Help engineers learn Python + Automation (Netmiko, Nornir, Ansible, etc.) using real multi-vendor labs (Nokia, Cisco, Huawei style) so they can work smarter and faster.
I’m doing this because I see many engineers struggling to move from manual config to automation.
I need your support:

Honest feedback on what you want to learn
Testing some labs
Recommendations on features that would make this tool actually useful in real jobs

If the platform becomes successful, I’ll give early contributors lifetime access + recognition.
Would you be open to a quick 10-minute call or just share your biggest automation challenges?
Looking forward to your thoughts.
Best regards,
Brian
Building NetForge Academy

Django==5.1.4
django-bootstrap5==24.3
psycopg2-binary==2.9.10
requests==2.32.3
python-dotenv==1.0.1
djangorestframework==3.15.2
django-crispy-forms==2.3
crispy-bootstrap5==0.7
gunicorn==23.0.0

import os
from pathlib import Path
from dotenv import load_dotenv

load_dotenv()

BASE_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent

SECRET_KEY = os.getenv('SECRET_KEY', 'netforge-2026-brian-nairobi')

DEBUG = True
ALLOWED_HOSTS = ['*']

INSTALLED_APPS = [
    'django.contrib.admin',
    'django.contrib.auth',
    'django.contrib.contenttypes',
    'django.contrib.sessions',
    'django.contrib.messages',
    'django.contrib.staticfiles',
    
    # Third party
    'bootstrap5',
    'crispy_forms',
    'crispy_bootstrap5',
    'rest_framework',
    
    # Local apps
    'users',
    'academy',
    'courses',
    'labs',
    'payments',
]

MIDDLEWARE = [
    'django.middleware.security.SecurityMiddleware',
    'django.contrib.sessions.middleware.SessionMiddleware',
    'django.middleware.common.CommonMiddleware',
    'django.middleware.csrf.CsrfViewMiddleware',
    'django.contrib.auth.middleware.AuthenticationMiddleware',
    'django.contrib.messages.middleware.MessageMiddleware',
    'django.middleware.clickjacking.XFrameOptionsMiddleware',
]

ROOT_URLCONF = 'netforge.urls'

TEMPLATES = [
    {
        'BACKEND': 'django.template.backends.django.DjangoTemplates',
        'DIRS': [BASE_DIR / 'templates'],
        'APP_DIRS': True,
        'OPTIONS': {
            'context_processors': [
                'django.template.context_processors.debug',
                'django.template.context_processors.request',
                'django.contrib.auth.context_processors.auth',
                'django.contrib.messages.context_processors.messages',
            ],
        },
    },
]

WSGI_APPLICATION = 'netforge.wsgi.application'

DATABASES = {
    'default': {
        'ENGINE': 'django.db.backends.sqlite3',
        'NAME': BASE_DIR / 'db.sqlite3',
    }
}

LANGUAGE_CODE = 'en-us'
TIME_ZONE = 'Africa/Nairobi'
USE_TZ = True
STATIC_URL = '/static/'
STATIC_ROOT = BASE_DIR / 'staticfiles'
MEDIA_URL = '/media/'
MEDIA_ROOT = BASE_DIR / 'media'

DEFAULT_AUTO_FIELD = 'django.db.models.BigAutoField'

CRISPY_ALLOWED_TEMPLATE_PACKS = "bootstrap5"
CRISPY_TEMPLATE_PACK = "bootstrap5"

from django.db import models
from django.contrib.auth.models import User
from django.utils import timezone

class Profile(models.Model):
    user = models.OneToOneField(User, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    phone_number = models.CharField(max_length=15, blank=True)
    is_student = models.BooleanField(default=True)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)

    def __str__(self):
        return self.user.username


class Course(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    description = models.TextField()
    level = models.CharField(max_length=50, choices=[
        ('beginner', 'Beginner'),
        ('intermediate', 'Intermediate'),
        ('advanced', 'Advanced'),
        ('isp', 'ISP Level')
    ])
    duration_hours = models.IntegerField(default=10)
    price = models.IntegerField(default=0)  # in KSh
    is_active = models.BooleanField(default=True)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)

    def __str__(self):
        return self.title


class Lab(models.Model):
    course = models.ForeignKey(Course, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='labs')
    title = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    description = models.TextField()
    topology_type = models.CharField(max_length=100, default="nokia_single")
    difficulty = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=[
        ('easy', 'Easy'), ('medium', 'Medium'), ('hard', 'Hard')
    ])
    estimated_time = models.IntegerField(default=30)  # minutes

    def __str__(self):
        return self.title


class Enrollment(models.Model):
    user = models.ForeignKey(User, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    course = models.ForeignKey(Course, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    enrolled_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    progress = models.IntegerField(default=0)  # percentage

    class Meta:
        unique_together = ('user', 'course')


class LabSession(models.Model):
    user = models.ForeignKey(User, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    lab = models.ForeignKey(Lab, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    started_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)
    completed_at = models.DateTimeField(null=True, blank=True)
    score = models.IntegerField(null=True, blank=True)

    def __str__(self):
        return f"{self.user.username} - {self.lab.title}"

from django.contrib import admin from django.urls import path, include from django.http import HttpResponse def home(request): return HttpResponse("""

Welcome to NetForge Academy
Network Automation Training Platform

Admin | Academy

""") urlpatterns = [ path('admin/', admin.site.urls), path('', home, name='home'), path('academy/', include('academy.urls')), ]

from django.urls import path
from . import views

urlpatterns = [
    path('', views.dashboard, name='dashboard'),
]

from django.db import models
from django.contrib.auth.models import User
from django.utils import timezone

class Profile(models.Model):
    user = models.OneToOneField(User, on_delete=models.CASCADE)
    phone_number = models.CharField(max_length=15, blank=True)
    is_student = models.BooleanField(default=True)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)

    def __str__(self):
        return self.user.username


class Course(models.Model):
    title = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    description = models.TextField()
    level = models.CharField(max_length=50, choices=[
        ('beginner', 'Beginner'),
        ('intermediate', 'Intermediate'),
        ('advanced', 'Advanced'),
        ('isp', 'ISP Level')
    ])
    duration_hours = models.IntegerField(default=10)
    price = models.IntegerField(default=0)  # in KSh
    is_active = models.BooleanField(default=True)
    created_at = models.DateTimeField(auto_now_add=True)

    def __str__(self):
        return self.title


class Lab(models.Model):
    course = models.ForeignKey(Course, on_delete=models.CASCADE, related_name='labs')
    title = models.CharField(max_length=200)
    description = models.TextField()
    topology_type = models.CharField(max_length=100, default="nokia_single")
    difficulty = models.CharField(max_length=20, choices=[
        ('easy', 'Easy'), ('medium', 'Medium'), ('hard', 'Hard')
    ])
    estimated_time = models.IntegerField(default=30)  # minutes

class Enrollment(models.Model):

    score = models.IntegerField(null=True, blank=True)

        return f"{self.user.username} - {self.lab.title}"
